specific_mechanism: Stomatal aperture is regulated by guard cells that integrate multiple environmental signals (blue light, CO2 concentration, vapor pressure deficit, ABA hormone) into ion fluxes (K+, Cl-, malate) that change turgor pressure and hence aperture. The integration is multi-input (4-6 signals), continuous (graded aperture), cheap (no central controller), reversible (can re-open in seconds), and has a defined arbitration rulebook (ABA dominates under drought, blue light dominates at dawn).
llm_application: Multi-signal context aperture for LLM agents: integrate four-to-six operational signals (compute budget, latency budget, safety signal, freshness signal, anomaly rate) into a single graded scalar aperture that controls context window admission. Distinct from attention masks (static, binary), from continuous gating (single-signal), and from explicit budget controllers (slow, central). Cheap, always-on, multi-input, reversible aperture regulator.
novelty_claim: Tool Attention (arxiv 2604.21816) implements dynamic tool-gating with state-aware gating function and Intent Schema Overlap scoring — directly the dynamic-gating-for-LLM-agents space. Silicon Mirror (2604.00478) implements behavioral-access-control gating based on real-time sycophancy risk. Multi-Layer Memory (2603.29194) has adaptive retrieval gating with retention regularization. Centralized budget control (2511.02755) addresses budget-aware multi-agent control. The multi-signal-integration architecture I propose is structurally similar to all four; the stomatal-aperture framing is a relabeling.
